meta.title: Enterprise Reusable Components: When and Why to Centralize
meta.description: Enterprise architecture case studies for centralization decisions

function.primary_purpose: Extract data from insurance policy documents across multiple business teams
key_capabilities: Parse policy numbers, effective dates, coverage limits
Extract customer PII with consistent data classification
Generate audit trails for regulatory compliance
Standardize data format across consuming systems

centralization_benefits: Uniform data classification and security controls
Centralized audit logging for regulatory examination
Consistent interpretation of legal and contractual terms
cost_of_inconsistency: GDPR breaches from inconsistent PII handling
SOX violations from different financial data processing
Customer disputes from conflicting data interpretation

centralize_when: Subject to industry-specific regulations (HIPAA, SOX, GDPR)
Audit trail consistency required for compliance
Data classification standards must be uniformly applied

function.primary_purpose: Provide standardized LLM access with cost control, security, and compliance governance
key_capabilities: Centralized API key management and rotation
Budget enforcement per cost center and project
Data classification validation before LLM calls
Complete audit trail for regulatory compliance

centralization_benefits: Centralized budget enforcement per cost center
Automatic model optimization (cheaper models for simple tasks)
Prompt caching and deduplication for cost savings
Bulk pricing negotiations with LLM providers
cost_of_inconsistency: Uncontrolled AI spend reaching $50K+ per team monthly
GDPR/HIPAA violations through uncontrolled API usage
No audit trail for regulatory compliance
Security vulnerabilities in distributed API key management
No visibility into AI ROI for executive management

centralize_when: Multiple teams using expensive LLM services
Regulatory compliance for data handling required
Executive visibility into AI spend needed
decentralize_when: Specialized model requirements not supported centrally
Need for bleeding-edge features from providers
